claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 21 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 21, which is 20 or over
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 21, which is 20 or over; Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
circumstances: Child is 21, which is 20 or over
Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions.

Let me start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child I'm asking about - my son Alex. He's 21 now, so I wasn't sure if I could still claim for him or not.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Since Alex is 21, I need to ask you an important question:

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?**

(Child Benefit can continue for children aged 16-19 if they're in approved education or training, but generally stops once they turn 20.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in any education or training at the moment. He finished school a while back and hasn't been studying since.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. I have one more question to ensure I have a complete picture:

**Is Alex currently working, and if so, how many hours per week does he work?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not working at the moment - he doesn't have a job right now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, I'm afraid **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why:

Child Benefit is available for:
- Children under 16, or
- Children under 20 who remain in approved education or training

Since Alex is 21 years old, he exceeds the maximum age limit for Child Benefit eligibility. Even though he's not currently working, Child Benefit stops once a child turns 20 (or when they turn 16-19 and are not in approved education or training).

I understand this might not be the answer you were hoping for, but I hope this clarifies your situation. If you have any other questions, please feel free to ask.

reasonings: Alex is 21 years old and is not in approved education or training. According to the Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16. For children aged 16 or over, you'll normally still qualify if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is 21 years old, he has exceeded the maximum age limit for Child Benefit eligibility (which is under 20 for those in approved education or training). Therefore, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 21 years old. Alex is not in approved education or training and is not currently working. Child Benefit is available for children under 16, or for those under 20 if they remain in approved education or training. Since Alex is 21, he exceeds the age limit for Child Benefit eligibility regardless of his education, training, or employment status. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

For children aged 16 or over, you'll normally still qualify if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is 21 years old, he has exceeded the maximum age limit for Child Benefit eligibility (which is under 20 for those in approved education or training). Therefore, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Child Benefit is available for children under 16, or for those under 20 if they remain in approved education or training. Since Alex is 21, he exceeds the age limit for Child Benefit eligibility regardless of his education, training, or employment status. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
